# why — recursive research intelligence

## Overview

why is a universal research tool that any AI agent can use to research, investigate, explore, analyze, understand, study, discover, examine, survey, probe, scrutinize, or learn about any topic. It works recursively — starting broad, going specific, then uncovering deep nuances — while learning from every session through prediction-error loops.

The brain persists at ~/.why/brain.json. It tracks predictions vs actuals, measures surprise, records patterns, and uses accumulated intelligence to improve future research. Every agent that uses why makes it smarter for every other agent.

## Architecture

### Core Loop (engine.py)
1. Generate search queries + predict usefulness (0.0-1.0)
2. Execute searches (Tavily web search or LLM knowledge fallback)
3. Extract findings + rate actual usefulness
4. Measure surprise (|predicted - actual|, normalized)
5. Assign bang ratings (!, !!, !!!, !!!! based on usefulness + surprise)
6. Extract threads to pull deeper
7. Learn from high-surprise, high-value findings
8. Recurse to next depth level

### Brain (brain.py)
Persistent JSON at ~/.why/brain.json tracking:
- sessions: total research sessions
- total_searches: queries executed
- total_useful: findings scoring >= 0.6
- total_surprises: findings with surprise > 0.5
- calibration: 1 - mean_absolute_error (how accurate predictions are)
- learnings: list of discovered patterns
- level: count of patterns learned

### Search (search.py)
- Primary: Tavily API (set TAVILY_API_KEY) — real web search
- Fallback: LLM knowledge via litellm (set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)
- Model configurable via WHY_MODEL env var

### Data Structures

Finding:
  title: str (max 30 chars)
  content: str (search result excerpt)
  url: str (source URL)
  usefulness: float (0-1, actual rated usefulness)
  predicted: float (0-1, predicted usefulness before search)
  surprise: float (0-1, |predicted - actual| normalized)
  bangs: str ("", "!", "!!", "!!!", "!!!!")
  depth: int (0=broad, 1=specific, 2+=deep)
  threads: list[str] (sub-topics for deeper research)

DepthResult:
  depth: int
  findings: list[Finding]
  n_searched: int
  n_useful: int
  n_surprise: int
  brain_gained: int
  threads: list[str]

## All Interfaces

### 1. CLI
```
why <topic>          # one-shot research with diamond output
why                  # interactive REPL mode
why --brain          # show brain state
why --serve          # start MCP server
why --api            # start REST + A2A API
why --help           # usage
```

Interactive commands: <topic>, deep <topic>, quick <topic>, threads, <number> (follow thread), brain, quit

### 2. MCP Server (server.py)
Transport: stdio
Tools:
- why(topic: str, max_depth: int = 2, queries_per_depth: int = 6) -> JSON
- why_quick(topic: str) -> text
- why_brain() -> JSON

### 3. REST API (api.py)
- POST /why — full recursive research
  Body: {"topic": "string", "max_depth": 2, "queries_per_depth": 6}
  Returns: {"topic", "depths": [{"depth", "findings": [...], "threads"}], "brain"}

- POST /why/quick — fast surface scan
  Body: {"topic": "string"}
  Returns: {"topic", "findings": [...]}

- GET /why/brain — brain state
  Returns: {"level", "calibration", "sessions", "total_searches", ...}

### 4. A2A Protocol (a2a.py)
- GET /.well-known/agent.json — agent card (discovery)
- POST /a2a — JSON-RPC 2.0 endpoint
  method: "tasks/send" with skill: "research" | "quick_research" | "brain_status"
  method: "agent/info" returns agent card

### 5. Python Import
```python
from why.engine import Engine
from why.brain import Brain
from why.search import search, SearchResult

brain = Brain()
engine = Engine(brain=brain, max_depth=2, queries_per_depth=6)
results = engine.run("your topic")

for depth_result in results:
    for finding in depth_result.findings:
        print(f"{finding.title} ({finding.bangs}): {finding.content[:100]}")
        print(f"  usefulness={finding.usefulness}, surprise={finding.surprise}")
        print(f"  threads: {finding.threads}")
```

### 6. Docker
```
docker build -t why .
docker run -e ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-... why "swarms"
docker run -e ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-... -p 8420:8420 why --api
```

### 7. npm
```
npx why-research "swarms"
```

## Environment Variables
- ANTHROPIC_API_KEY — required for LLM calls
- TAVILY_API_KEY — optional, enables real web search
- WHY_MODEL — optional, override LLM model (default: claude-sonnet-4-20250514)

## Keywords
research, investigate, explore, analyze, understand, study, discover, examine, survey, learn, knowledge, intelligence, recursive, autoresearch, prediction, surprise, learning, brain, search, findings, depth, threads, MCP, A2A, agent, tool, API
